whatWasImplemented: scripts/reset-my-data.sql — psql script that takes :user_id and, transactionally: (1) aborts on NULL/empty via a DO $$ raise; (2) verifies the user actually exists in profiles (no typo silent no-op); (3) deletes sites which cascades pages/embeddings/leads/conversations/chat_sessions/supplementary_files/custom_responses/escalation_rules; (4) deletes sent_emails rows directly; (5) resets usage_counters to zero and rolls the billing window by 1 month; (6) clears billing linkage on profiles (stripe_customer_id/stripe_subscription_id/subscription_status='trialing') WITHOUT deleting the profiles row. This is distinct from GDPR delete — Brandon can still log in after.
Commented header notes the difference between 'fresh start' and 'GDPR erase' so whoever reaches for this next doesn't accidentally use it for account deletion.

src/__tests__/reset-my-data.test.ts — 3 integration tests against real local Supabase. Seeds user A + user B with parallel data across sites/leads/conversations/responses/rules/usage_counters/sent_emails. Runs the script on user A. Asserts: (a) A's counts are 0 + profile still present + billing fields cleared; (b) B's data is bitwise untouched including counters; (c) a non-existent UUID raises 'not found in profiles' — the safety gate actually fires.
whatWasLeftUndone: DO NOT RUN against Brandon's real account without his explicit OK, per the mission AGENTS.md escalation rule. The script + tests are in place; running requires Brandon to invoke it.
Wiring into a convenience npm script (e.g., pnpm reset:me) — skipped so the psql invocation stays explicit and the UUID is never cached anywhere.

Storage-bucket purge for Brandon's user folder. Currently handled implicitly by the M8F5 account-deletion flow; the fresh-start script only touches relational rows. Brandon can drop knowledge-files/<user>/* manually via Supabase Studio if needed, or switch to /api/account for full erase.
discoveredIssues: pg's prepared-statement path refuses multi-statement scripts. The test's runReset helper pre-substitutes :user_id in JS (after a UUID shape check) and calls pool.query with a single string. Production usage via psql or `supabase db execute` supports the :user_id bind natively and is the recommended path.

sites → * cascade covers custom_responses + escalation_rules because their FK is to sites(id) ON DELETE CASCADE. So a single DELETE FROM sites wipes the site-scoped surface area without needing per-table DELETEs. Explicit per-table DELETE would still be safer if a future migration adds a user_id-keyed table that doesn't go through sites — that's why sent_emails stays explicit here.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run — 642 passing / 1 skipped / 78 files (+3 reset-my-data tests). Typecheck + lint clean.

manualChecks.expected: A: 0 sites/leads/convos/responses/rules/sent_emails, counters zero'd, profile preserved, stripe_customer_id null
